blocks.text: July 1
Woke up and went down the hill. The mission was to find a bike store to get new brakes installed on the front. I started really early. Like 5:30am.
I took some trails to avoid busy roads. It quickly turned into a really rough muddy trail with surprisingly mini lobsters in the trail. One pond had 100 of them.
When the pedals get wet my feet slip all over the place. Since I had crappy plastic pedals without grips. The mud from this trail once again made it almost impossible to keep my shoes on the pedals.
The mud from the trail was enough that my front wheel would no longer move. I had to clear out the mud by removing the wheels to fix it.

When I looked at the map the only bike store nearby was in this little town called Polla up the road. But it didn't open until 9 and it was 7. I would have to wait before taking the big mountain road.
I think this trail would suck if it rains, Of course it starts to rain.
I found a bar in Polla and sat down had coffe and waited for the bike shop to open.
I started a convo with two German/ Italians. Afterwards he payed for my breakfast!
Another Italian man comes in and asks about the bike, he was a big cyclist. He loved racing and all things about biking. We talked for a while right after
After our conversation he tries to pay for my breakfast too!
But it's already paid for by the other.
wow.
Then I go to bike store it's open.
He is helpful, he find me good pedals for a good deal. The mechanic was the sun of the owner of the shop. The dad comes in who is the owner.
We chat about the bike tour I'm doing as his son worked on my bike.
Then out of nowhere he walks in with something in his hands. He then processed to give me the cycling Jerry and padded shorts worth probably about 300 euros. I was in shock.

blocks.caption: Me and the bike store owner

blocks.text: They change the breaks and I bought new pedals that have better grip in wet conditions.
Right as I leave it stops raining and once again I return to the cafe. He closes for lunch and I go to super market.
It starts to rain again... I wait it out at the super market.
Finally I went back to the same bar as before one last time. He gives me a free coffee and I speed like a rocket ship over the final mountain before I arrive on the other side of Italy.
WOWOW again crazy down hill, and fun fast and easy uphill. In the racing cycling kit I felt so fast up the hill. I felt like the tiny twin of Polla had given me wings to fly over this mountain.

blocks.caption: Powered by coffe and the jersey

blocks.text: Take a look at some of the pictures I took. It only can capture a fraction of the beauty.
I got to the other side. Another moments where I look back at the mountains behind me and wonder how I did it.
As always the answer is just little by little, pedalling.
I then found a little gravel trail that let me dodge a highway on the map by accident.
It quickly become unridable from deep sand. At one point the trail complete disappeared and I was lost.
But at the end I found a river by accident.
I made the decision to stop early there and camp by the river instead of the ocean.
Enjoyed the river bank and cooked more pasta in the middle of nowhere. I didnt have service so I missed a day of posting these blogs.
here is the picture of where I camped.
Bye for now,
Erik
